extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires exactly when all five of {qt_prolongation, hepatic_impairment, seizure_history, heart_failure, low_ejection_fraction} are true; otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false. rec_drug_b is set true only by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, condition qt_prolongation), but guard_suppress_b (priority 90) fires under the identical full five-way conjunction and, having strictly higher priority, forces rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a is true (full conjunction holds), the guard also matches and dominates the trigger, making rec_drug_b false. Conversely, whenever rec_drug_b is true, the full conjunction is not satisfied, so rec_a_from_conjunction does not fire and rec_drug_a is false. The two outputs can never be jointly true, so the mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire input space.
